log.did: Strength A; tested TGU at 20 lb — too heavy, dropping back to 17.5
log.notes: Post-workout ~3/10 anterior + superior left shoulder — confirmed average / baseline (not a flare). 20 lb TGU test failed; hold at 17.5, retest in 2 weeks. AM next-day check: 0–1/10, much better.
1/10 anterior shoulder burning during run, very mild. AM next-day check (4/30): 1/10, felt good overall.

log.did: Strength B as prescribed. Prone Y-T-W @ 5 lb DBs; Cossack squat @ 17.5 lb KB; ATG/Bulgarian split squat @ 15 lb KB; single-leg RDL @ 17.5 lb KB.
log.notes: Loads above week-1 prescription on Cossack (17.5 KB vs BW) and split squat (15 KB vs BW); single-leg RDL 17.5 vs prescribed 20. Workout went well. AM next-day check (5/1): zero pain.

2026-05-01.why_today: Friday sits between yesterday's Strength B and tomorrow's Long Day — the lightest slot of the week, by design. Today is a deliberate offload, not a missed session.
2026-05-01.evidence: Polarized training research (Seiler, Foster) consistently shows that low-intensity recovery days protect the quality of hard days; cutting them out reduces output on the workouts that actually drive adaptation, and ACWR drift into the injury-risk band starts when tissue load piles up without these valves.
2026-05-01.building_toward: Phase 1 base + shoulder. Mobility today keeps the runner anti-tightening work and the PT shoulder staples (band ER, foam roller t-spine) in weekly rotation without adding load — small deposits, big interest by mid-July.

2026-05-02.why_today: Saturday is the long-day slot — your highest-volume session of the week, placed after Friday's offload so you come in fresh. Week A means a long hilly hike.
2026-05-02.evidence: Extended low-intensity work (2–4 hr, conversational pace) builds aerobic base and connective-tissue durability without the eccentric stress that climbing and strength days impose on the shoulder. The progressive pack loading (bodyweight → 25 lb over weeks) adds capacity without spiking ACWR.
2026-05-02.building_toward: Aerobic floor for the December marathon goal and general climbing endurance. Once 4 weeks pass symptom-free, Week B (tension board + easy run) becomes the default and you move to 2× climbing/wk — the hike weeks are earning that transition.
